summary: Fixed shared pet access (#102) — backend only. Two changes to backend/src/models/pet.ts: (1) Added optional user_role field to the Pet interface. (2) Updated findPetsForUser query to SELECT p.*, po.role as user_role instead of p.* alone, so each pet in the list response carries the authenticated user's role for that pet. The GET /pets route handler already returns the raw query result via res.json(pets), so user_role propagates to the API response with no route handler changes required. No frontend files touched. TypeScript compilation clean.
notes_for_reviewer: accepted_at IS NOT NULL filter in findPetsForUser is correct — addPetOwner always sets accepted_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP at insert time, so all access rows (both owner-created and invitation-accepted) have a non-null accepted_at.

user_role is typed as optional (string | null | undefined) on the Pet interface since other code paths that return Pet objects (findPetById, updatePet, etc.) do not join pet_owners and will leave user_role undefined. Only findPetsForUser populates it.

The GET /pets/:id route handler already fetches role separately via getUserPetRole and merges it as userRole (camelCase) in the response. The list endpoint uses snake_case user_role from the SQL alias — consistent with the rest of the JSON payload which uses snake_case.
